name: claims_adjudication
description: a health-insurance claims adjudication world with claim states; actions submit, review, approve, deny

card.description: a health-insurance claims adjudication world with claim states; actions submit, review, approve, deny

rules: A claim enters the world via `submit`, starting in the `submitted` state.
`approve` moves the oldest `under_review` claim to `approved` and pays its amount.
`deny` moves the oldest `under_review` claim to `denied`; nothing is paid.
A claim must be reviewed before it can be approved or denied; a `submitted` claim cannot be approved or denied directly.
`approved` and `denied` are terminal states; a claim never leaves them.
An action whose targeted eligible claim does not exist is a no-op.
Without an explicit `claim_id`, each action acts on the oldest eligible claim, so the queue is adjudicated first-in, first-out.

emit.report: Claims adjudication summary: {total_claims} claim(s) total -- {submitted_count} submitted, {under_review_count} under review, {approved_count} approved (${total_approved_amount} paid), {denied_count} denied.

objectives.name: adjudicate
objectives.goal: maximize approved_count + denied_count (reach a final decision)
